title: Powerhouse uses header signing (document ID = cryptographic signature of creator) and action signing (every mutation signed with ECDSA P-256)
content: Header signing: when a document is created, `documentType + createdAtUtcIso + nonce` is signed with the creator's private key and the resulting signature becomes the document's `id`. The header embeds the public key (JWK) for self-contained verification via `validateHeader()`. Action signing: each dispatched action produces a 5-element Signature tuple `[timestamp, signerKey, actionHash, hashField, signatureHex]`. The `actionHash` is SHA-1 of `documentId + scope + actionType + JSON(input)`. The signed message uses prefix `\x19Signed Operation:\n{length}` matching Ethereum-style signing. Both use ECDSA with P-256 curve and SHA-256 via Web Crypto API.
description: Document ID is the creator's signature over header fields; each action gets a 5-tuple Signature with did:key.
